reviews: I recommend him for Chem 1031 because the curves on the exams are helpful: if the class median exam grade was 65%, and you got an 80%, that's nearly a 100% after the curve. But his teaching can be rough and hard to follow.

Bloxton's class seemed overwhelming at first, but once you understand what to expect it is a walk in the park. If you go to every single lecture, I guarantee you will not fail the class and will succeed. He goes through his practice exams which are 99% similar to his own exams. Go into his office hours and he will help break down concepts. Nice guy

There was a decent amount of homework but if you don't procrastinate like I did its not too much. I only studied for one exam, I don't think his exams are too hard. However if you are the type of student that needs to study more or wants an A he gives a lot of good resources. Gives a lot of good info and prepares you for tests + questions on test.

I love stem but he made me hate it. Not to scare anyone but I had to teach myself the whole class. He is really nice but the class is very fast pace and easy to fall behind. Maybe it was just me but no matter how hard I studied, I barely made it through the course. I took him 2 sems and idk what he did the 2nd but it was rough. Good luck

Dr. Bloxton is a really kind and dedicated professor. You can tell he cares about his students. He doesn't assign a ton of grades, mostly just exams and the final, but he offers a lot of extra credit, which is great. I'd recommend participating in recitation so he gets to know you better. Overall, a fantastic guy and a class I'd recommend!

Bloxton gave so much extra credit in this class it was genuinely hard to fail. There was like a 17% curve on one exam and he just gave extra credit randomly if you showed effort. Attendance isn't graded but he gives extra credit if you go. He is really funny and cares a lot, he just wants people to try.

genuinely just read the textbook, do the practice exams and youll be fine, lectures were boring but i went for poll everywhere, and his midterms were loaded with extra credit, only took 4 quizzes in recitation (week before midterm) they were more difficult than the midterm bc theres no practice questions, cool enough professor

It's not funny to waste thousands of dollars because you were forced to take his class. He does not take making a test seriously. I take my career very seriously he doesn't. I cannot believe he made this the right answer on an exam:
D. None of these choices are correct according to Bart! Do you believe Bart?

Genuinely started scoring higher on tests once I stopped showing up to his lectures, which are honestly just timewasting simulators. His lectures are hard to follow since he insists on not using powerpoints. He also gives extra credit almost exclusively to people who get 100% on assessments

Dr. B is interesting, he's a funny guy, he just can't keep people's attention in class. People stopped going to chem lecture at 8am and recitation so he made attendance mandatory, the people that went to class got more extra credit. Homework takes forever, 4 exams, difficult but you can pass if you know what you're doing, study, and you'll be fine!

Took him for 1 and 2 and he got worse. He's super nice but if you aren't naturally good at chem you will struggle. I went to his lectures but they are a waste of time bc he literally just reads his notes that are already provided to us the textbook. He does give practice exams that are super helpful which he goes over during class.
Very diffiucult class test heavy if you are not a genius you will not pass this class.

Dr. Bloxton is a great professor! He cares about students understanding the information and emphasizes important topics that will be on tests. My semester had 5 tests which included the cumulative final. Although chemistry is hard and he has high expectations for students, I ended with a 97%. Attend class and you will do fine! Also he's hilarious!
Please be prepared to teach yourself. Completely outdated.

Lectures can be hard to follow and it can sometimes be hard to understand what he's saying. He gives extra credit opportunities and wants his students so succeed in the class, but the tests are hard and a huge chunk of your grade. You'll likely spend lots of time teaching yourself concepts from his class.

Dr. Bloxton was a great professor for General Chemistry! He genuinely cares about students succeeding. He has regular office hours and is always willing to answer questions. He also makes it clear when certain topics are important and will be tested on if you pay attention in his lectures.

One of the best professors I've had in the science department. He truly cares about students passing and offers plenty of extra credit opportunities. Sometimes curves the grades, which really helps. His lectures can be fun and engaging. I would definitely recommend taking his class!

All I gotta say is you better start studying the first day of classes make sure you pass all the tests, because that final exam is crazy. Make sure you get 95-100 on hw 100 in participation every point matters and do all the extra credit you will pass. He's the best chem professor out of everyone, make sure to see him regularly so he knows you.
All I gotta say is RUN. AS FAST AS YOU CAN.

This professor's lectures were not very helpful, so make sure to review outside of class. The exams were just like the practices posted, which helped during the exam. He is a nice guy and works out the problems on the board. Overall, he was a good professor but work needs to be done outside of class.
